tagline: Five was enough back then. Now it's just dust.
liner: Step into a gritty East Coast boom bap world at 92 BPM, where dusty drums with heavy swing crack against thick vinyl hiss and foghorn sub-bass shadows. A sinister minor-key piano sets the noir mood while bright xylophone melodies doubled on mellow flute weave through raw confessional vocals from a raspy male lead. Gruff call-and-response ad-libs contrast sharply with a smooth, smoky R&B chorus delivering passionate vibrato in layered harmonies against muted trumpet and eerie synth motifs. This track is a melancholic reflection on how inflation devalues money while destroying community bonds, capturing nostalgia for an era where trust mattered more than price.
worldCaption: Dusty vinyl hiss meets sinister piano; bright xylophone weaves through smoky R&B harmonies against foghorn bass in this gritty noir landscap

bios.lead: Raspy voice confesses the cost of inflation with raw honesty.
bios.drums: Keeps the floor shaking. Never misses a downbeat or crack.
bios.other: Weaves flute, trumpet shadows, and synth motifs into the fog.
